mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easing financial conditions that lowers equity discount rates and lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of months with a 1-month FEDFUNDS decline, or if the rolling 3-year sign hit rate falls below 0.52.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its recent pace while FEDFUNDS is already elevated, real rates and term-premium fears rise, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above 3% while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the interaction adds no incremental predictive value versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or larger rise in UNRATE over three months signals labor-market deterioration that weakens earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in triggered periods, or if the sign accuracy stays below 0.55 over any rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth above its recent trend confirms labor income momentum and broad demand strength, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS growth periods fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if rolling rank correlation is below 0.03 for 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad final demand and housing-linked spending momentum, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform each series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of instances where both series are above their 6-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO over the prior month indicates improving industrial supply and productivity conditions that support earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly increases in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in GDPC1 over the latest quarterly reading signals a demand slowdown that should depress equity earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines from the prior quarterly release
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the sign hit rate stays below 0.52 in rolling 4-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with rising CPIAUCSL creates a real-rate and policy-pressure shock that should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.25% and CPIAUCSL 1-month change positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered 5-day SPX returns are not negative more than 60% of the time, or if the interaction term does not outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward tests.
